MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-11-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Rafal Ofierski, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY 554-4721, for defendants San Francisco
Library Commission, Commissioners Charles Higueras, Carol Steiman, Lonnie Chin and
Helen Bautista, moving for summary judgment
Robert J. Moskowitz, Esq. of LAW OFFICES OF STANLEY G. HILTON 439-4893, for
plaintiff and opposing party

RULING:
A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- DEFENDANTs SAN FRANCISCO
LIBRARY COMMISSION, Commissioners Charles Higueras, Carol Steiman, Lonnie Chin and
Helen Bautista's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT -- Granted; no triable issue of fact
re: compliance with Brown Act and Sunshine Ordinance Requirement that commission
provide public with opportunity to comment before taking action on any agenda item. (DAG/301/KG-X) Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR 4780

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-18-2002 Set For Trial On May-12-03 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Apr-25-03 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 514

Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-16-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Stanley Goumas Hilton, Esq. 439-4893 for Plaintiff petitioning for a preliminary injunction
Rafal Ofierski, Deputy City Attorney 554-4721 for Respondents SAN FRANCISCO LIBRARY COMMISSION, et al. opposing party
* A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- Plaintiff's petition for preliminary injunction is denied -- no showing of irreparable harm or likelihood of success on merits. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Hon. A. JAMES ROBERTSON II (301/KH) Eric Throne, CSR 7855
